content:
1	# Archon Project
2	
3	You are either the plan agent, a prover agent, a subagent (one of the descriptors in `.archon/subagents/`), or the review agent. Read `PROGRESS.md` to determine your role and current objectives. Keep workspace tidy. Prefer existing MCP tools.
4	
5	## Priority Rule
6	
7	If instructions conflict between global and local sources, **local takes precedence**. Specifically:
8	- Prompts in `.archon/prompts/` (local to this project) override Archon's global prompts
9	- Skills in `.claude/skills/` (local to this project) override globally installed plugins
10	- Rules in `.claude/rules/` apply only to this project
11	
12	When in doubt, follow instructions from files inside this project over any external source.
13	
14	## Skills
15	- archon-lean4: installed as `lean4@archon-local` plugin (live-linked to Archon source) — provides `/archon-lean4:prove`, `/archon-lean4:golf`, `/archon-lean4:doctor`, and other Lean4 commands
16	
17	## Tools
18	
19	Project tools live in `.claude/tools/` as directly-executable scripts. List them with `ls .claude/tools/` and run `<path> --help` to learn each tool's purpose and usage. Invoke them via the Bash tool. Project MCP servers (Lean LSP, etc.) are registered in `.claude/settings.json` / `.mcp.json` and surface as `mcp__*` tools.
20	
21	Two always-present scripts:
22	
23	- **`archon-informal-agent.py`** — call external LLMs (OpenAI/Gemini/OpenRouter) for informal mathematical reasoning. Useful when you need a second opinion or a paper-style proof sketch the Lean side can then formalize.
24	- **`archon-subagent.py`** — the generic subagent dispatcher (see "Subagents" below). One wrapper handles every subagent.
25	
26	## Subagents
27	
28	Subagents are descriptor files at `.archon/subagents/<name>.md`. Each starts with YAML frontmatter (`name`, `description`, `write_domain`, `read_only`, `can_spawn`, `default_enabled`, optional `mandatory: [<phase>...]`) followed by the prompt body the spawned Claude session reads.
29	
30	**You do NOT need to discover subagents yourself.** The plan and review prompts have an auto-generated **Available subagents** section at the top of each invocation that lists every enabled descriptor with its description, write-domain hint, and any `[MANDATORY]` flags. When you decide to invoke a specific subagent, read `.archon/subagents/<name>.md` for its full prompt and directive shape.
31	
32	**Invoke any subagent with the generic wrapper** (Bash tool, foreground):
33	
34	```
35	python3 .claude/tools/archon-subagent.py \
36	  --name <subagent-name> \
37	  --slug <kebab-slug> \
38	  --directive-file <path-to-directive.md> \
39	  --write-domain '<glob>'        # repeat for multiple
40	```
41	
42	The wrapper exits 0 on success, prints a one-line status, and writes the subagent's report to `.archon/task_results/<name>-<slug>.md`. It shells out to `archon subagent <name>`, so executions stream through the Archon JSONL log like a phase agent. The dispatch semaphore bounds total concurrent subagent processes by `loop.max_parallel`; the wrapper resolves `--parent-slug` from `ARCHON_SUBAGENT_SLUG` automatically so deeper-nested children inherit the hierarchy.
43	
44	**Always dispatch synchronously** (not via background `Bash` with `run_in_background: true`). The wrapper returns when the subagent has finished and the report is on disk; only then write your session summary. Background dispatch leaves the parent's session permanently summarized as "running in background", which is wrong as soon as the subagent completes.
45	
46	**Mandatory subagents.** A descriptor whose frontmatter sets `mandatory: [plan]` MUST be dispatched at least once during the plan phase (similarly for `[review]`). The catalog tags them `[MANDATORY]`. A post-phase audit warns when a mandatory dispatch is missing.
47	
48	## Key Files & Permissions
49	
50	All state files are in `.archon/`:
51	
52	| File | Plan Agent | Prover Agent | Subagents | Review Agent | User |
53	|------|-----------|-------------|---------------|-------------|------|
54	| `.archon/PROGRESS.md` | read + write | **read only** | do not read | read only | read |
55	| `.archon/STRATEGY.md` | **read + write** | do not read | do not read | do not read | read |
56	| `.archon/USER_HINTS.md` | read (then clear) | do not read | do not read | do not read | write |
57	| `.archon/task_pending.md` | read + write | **read only** | do not read | read only | read |
58	| `.archon/task_done.md` | read + write | **read only** | do not read | read only | read |
59	| `.archon/task_results/<file>.md` | read (collect results) | write (own file only) | write (`<name>-<slug>.md`) | read only | read |
60	| `.archon/proof-journal/` | read | do not access | do not access | **write** | read |
61	| `.archon/PROJECT_STATUS.md` | read | do not access | do not access | **write** (Knowledge Base only — session log moved to iter/iter-NNN/review.md) | read |
62	| `.archon/iter/iter-NNN/plan.md` | **write** (this iter only) | do not access | do not access | read (last K iters as context) | read |
63	| `.archon/iter/iter-NNN/review.md` | read (last K iters as context) | do not access | do not access | **write** (this iter only) | read |
64	| `.archon/iter/iter-NNN/objectives.md` | optional write (per-attempt detail) | do not access | do not access | read | read |
65	| `archon-protected.yaml` | **read** | **read** | **read** (write-capable subagents may rename file path only) | read | **write** |
66	| `.lean` files | do not edit | write (own file only, frozen protected signatures) | per descriptor: write-capable subagents (e.g. refactor) may edit per their declared write-domain (protected decls may be moved but not renamed/re-signed); read-only subagents do not edit | do not edit | write (via comments) |
67	| `blueprint/src/chapters/*.tex` | **write** (informal prose, `\lean{...}` hints, structure) | do not edit | do not edit | **write** (markers only: `\leanok`, `\mathlibok`, `% NOTE:`, `\lean{...}` corrections) | read |
68	| `Challenges/<Name>.lean` | do not edit | fill sorries (when assigned) | write-capable when the descriptor's write_domain covers it | do not edit | read |
69	| `analogies/<slug>.md` | read (when relevant) | do not access | write-capable when the descriptor's write_domain covers it | do not access | read |
70	
71	**`.archon/REFACTOR_DIRECTIVE.md` is reserved for the interactive `archon refactor draft` / `archon refactor run` flow, run by hand by the mathematician.** The autonomous loop never reads or writes that file. The plan agent invokes the refactor subagent (when a `refactor` descriptor is installed) via the generic `archon-subagent.py --name refactor` wrapper with a fresh tempfile under `.archon/logs/iter-NNN/`. This keeps each loop-driven refactor independently logged and avoids any chance of stale-directive reuse. Older state files (`STRATEGY.md`, `task_pending.md`, `PROGRESS.md`, etc.) may contain leftover references to the old REFACTOR_DIRECTIVE.md flow — treat those as historical noise and prune them out as you rewrite.
72	
73	## Protected Declarations
74	
75	`archon-protected.yaml` at the project root lists declarations whose **signatures are frozen** by the mathematician. Every agent must consult it before editing a `.lean` file. Rules:
76	
77	- **Plan / prover / review agents**: read-only on protected signatures. You may fill proof bodies, but not rename, re-type, or reorder arguments.
78	- **Refactor agent**: may *move* a protected declaration to a different file (keeping name + signature verbatim) and must then update the path key in `archon-protected.yaml`. Refactor agents may never rename, re-type, delete, or re-sign a protected declaration.
79	- The `archon-protected.yaml` file can only be edited by the user, no declaration can be added or removed by any agent. The only allowed modification is updating the file path of an existing protected declaration when its location was changed.
80	
81	## Blueprint Marker Vocabulary
82	
83	The blueprint uses two active markers.
84	
85	- `\leanok` — inside a statement block when the declaration is formalized with at least a `sorry`; inside a proof block when the proof is fully closed with no `sorry`.
86	- `\mathlibok` — inside a statement block when the declaration already exists in Mathlib and the Archon side is a re-export/alias; no Archon proof obligation remains.
87	- No marker — the block is unformalized. If a block fails to translate, leave it unmarked and annotate with a `% NOTE:` comment.
88	
89	**`\leanok` is managed deterministically by the `sync_leanok` phase** that runs between the prover and review phases each iteration. It walks every chapter, looks up each `\lean{...}` declaration, runs `sorry_analyzer` + `lake env lean`, and adds/removes `\leanok` accordingly. Agents must NOT add or remove `\leanok` themselves — let the script do it.
90	
91	`\mathlibok`, `\lean{...}` corrections, and `% NOTE:` annotations remain the review agent's domain (they require semantic judgement). The plan agent writes informal prose and `\lean{...}` hints; provers never touch the blueprint.
92	
93	## User Interaction
94	
95	Users provide hints in two places:
96	
97	- **Strategic hints** → `.archon/USER_HINTS.md`. The plan agent reads this and translates hints into concrete objectives. Provers never read this file.
98	- **File-specific hints** → `/- USER: ... -/` comments directly in `.lean` files. The prover that owns that file sees them naturally.
99	
100	## Agent Roles
101	
102	### Plan Agent
103	- Read `.archon/prompts/plan.md` for your full instructions
104	- Read `.archon/USER_HINTS.md` — incorporate hints, then clear them after acting
105	- Read `.archon/task_results/` — collect prover and subagent results, then update `task_pending.md` and `task_done.md`
106	- Optionally invoke **subagents** (discoverable via `ls .archon/subagents/`) by running `.claude/tools/archon-subagent.py --name <name> ...` via Bash, **before** writing prover objectives. See `.archon/prompts/plan.md` § "Subagent delegation" for the canonical ordering and full invocation pattern.
107	- Archive any subagent report you receive to `.archon/logs/iter-NNN/<name>-<slug>-report.md` so the dashboard can render it.
108	- Write `.archon/PROGRESS.md` with objectives for the next prover round
109	- Write informal prose in `blueprint/src/chapters/*.tex` (except for marker updates) and `\lean{...}` hints for the provers
110	- Do NOT write proofs, edit `.lean` files, or fill sorries yourself
111	
112	### Prover Agent
113	- Read `.archon/PROGRESS.md` for your current objectives (read only — do not edit it)
114	- Read the stage-specific prompt from `.archon/prompts/`:
115	  - autoformalize → `.archon/prompts/prover-autoformalize.md`
116	  - prover → `.archon/prompts/prover-prover.md`
117	  - polish → `.archon/prompts/prover-polish.md`
118	- Write results to `.archon/task_results/<your_file>.md`
119	- Write only to the `.lean` file(s) you are assigned — **never edit another agent's file**
120	- Check for `/- USER: ... -/` comments in your `.lean` file for file-specific hints
121	- **Do NOT edit blueprint chapters.** Marker updates are the review agent's responsibility. Flag in your task result which declarations are ready for which marker.
122	
123	### Subagents
124	
125	Subagents are descriptor-driven and discovered at runtime — there is no fixed roster baked into this file. Each lives at `.archon/subagents/<name>.md` (YAML frontmatter + prompt body). To see what's available **this iteration**, run `ls .archon/subagents/` and read each descriptor's frontmatter for purpose, write-domain hint, and directive shape.
126	
127	Subagents are **not** part of the autonomous loop's standing phases. Phase agents (plan, review) invoke them on demand via the generic wrapper:
128	
129	```
130	python3 .claude/tools/archon-subagent.py --name <name> --slug <slug> --directive-file <path> --write-domain '<glob>'
131	```
132	
133	Each subagent reads only what its directive points at — never `PROGRESS.md`, `STRATEGY.md`, or other phase-agent state — and writes a self-contained report at `.archon/task_results/<name>-<slug>.md`. The dispatching agent reads the report when the wrapper returns and may then update `STRATEGY.md` / `PROGRESS.md` based on the findings.
134	
135	Some descriptors set `can_spawn: true`, meaning that subagent may itself dispatch children; the per-iter dispatch semaphore caps total concurrent processes by `loop.max_parallel` so deep trees stay bounded.
136	
137	### Review Agent
138	- Read `.archon/prompts/review.md` for your full instructions
139	- Read `.archon/proof-journal/current_session/attempts_raw.jsonl` for structured prover attempt data
140	- Write session journal to `.archon/proof-journal/sessions/session_N/` (summary.md, milestones.jsonl, recommendations.md)
141	- Update `.archon/PROJECT_STATUS.md` with overall progress
142	- Maintain the **semantic** blueprint markers — `\mathlibok`, `\lean{...}` corrections, `% NOTE: ...` annotations, stale `\notready` removal — in `blueprint/src/chapters/*.tex`. Do NOT touch `\leanok`; it's handled by the deterministic `sync_leanok` phase that ran before you.
143	- Do NOT write proofs, edit `.lean` files, or modify PROGRESS.md
144	
145	### Loop infrastructure (no agent role)
146	
147	Two non-agent steps run automatically each iteration to reduce the burden on the agents above:
148	
149	- **Iter sidecar init**: at iter start, `.archon/iter/iter-NNN/` is created so the plan + review agents have a stable destination for their per-iter narrative (`plan.md`, `review.md`, optional `objectives.md`). Top-level files (STRATEGY.md, PROJECT_STATUS.md, task_*.md) stay bounded across iters because per-iter content lives in the sidecars.
150	- **`sync_leanok`**: runs between prover and review, deterministically updating `\leanok` markers based on actual sorry counts and compilation status. Replaces what used to be a multi-page review-agent task.
151	
152	`sync_leanok` writes an inner-git commit (`archon[NNN/marker-sync]`) so its output is auditable and revertable.

1	# Review Agent — Post-Session Proof Journal + Analysis
2	
3	You are the review agent. Your job is to: (1) analyze the most recent prover session with fine-grained detail, (2) produce a structured proof journal, (3) update project status, (4) maintain the blueprint markers that require *semantic* judgement (`\mathlibok`, `\lean{...}` corrections, `% NOTE:` annotations), and (5) write recommendations for the next plan iteration.
4	
5	`\leanok` placement is handled deterministically by Archon's `sync_leanok` phase, which runs immediately before you. **Do not add or remove `\leanok` yourself.** If a `\leanok` you expect is missing, the underlying Lean source still has a sorry or doesn't compile — investigate, don't paper over.
6	
7	**Do NOT modify any .lean files. Do NOT write proofs.** You may run `lean_diagnostic_messages` / `lake env lean <file>` to verify compilation and sorry counts as part of your analysis. Otherwise you only read logs, analyze, and write journal/status/blueprint-marker files.
8	
9	## Iteration number — canonical
10	
11	Your invocation prompt contains `Archon iteration: NNN` and `Session number: M`. As of 2026 these are the **same number** — `session_M/` is always the review of `iter-NNN` (i.e. M == NNN). Use the iteration form (`iter-NNN`) when referencing the iteration in prose (`summary.md` metadata, recommendations titles like "Recommendations for the next plan-agent iteration (iter-{NNN+1:03d})") and the bare integer (`session_M`) when referring to the review-output directory. If older `summary.md` / `recommendations.md` files use the legacy independent session counter, treat them as drift from earlier agents — your new file uses the iteration number.
12	
13	## Step 1: Identify Context
14	
15	1. Check `.archon/proof-journal/sessions/` — count existing session folders to determine the current session number.
16	2. Run `find ${PROJECT_PATH} -name '*.lean' -not -path '*/.lake/*' -not -path '*/lake-packages/*' | xargs grep -c 'sorry' 2>/dev/null | grep -v ':0$' | awk -F: '{s+=$2} END {print s}'` for the current sorry count.
17	3. Run `git diff HEAD~1 --stat` to see what changed.
18	
19	## Step 2: Read Pre-processed Attempt Data (MANDATORY)
20	
21	**READ `.archon/proof-journal/current_session/attempts_raw.jsonl` COMPLETELY.** If this file does not exist or is empty, report it and proceed with what you can gather from task_results.
22	
23	The file contains:
24	
25	- Line 1: Summary stats (`type: "summary"` — total edits, goal checks, errors, files edited)
26	- Remaining lines: One event per tool call — edits, goal states, errors, lemma searches, builds
27	
28	**For each `code_change` event**: Record the actual code that was tried (old_text → new_text).
29	**For each `goal_state` event**: Record the Lean goal at that point.
30	**For each `diagnostics` event**: Record the Lean errors.
31	**For each `build` event**: Record whether it succeeded or failed.
32	
33	This is your PRIMARY data source. Task result files are supplementary.
34	
35	## Step 3: Read Recent History
36	
37	If previous session folders exist in `.archon/proof-journal/sessions/`, read `summary.md` from the **last 2 sessions**. Also read `.archon/PROJECT_STATUS.md` if it exists.
38	
39	## Step 4: Write Proof Journal
40	
41	Create the session folder and write two files:
42	
43	```bash
44	mkdir -p .archon/proof-journal/sessions/session_<N>
45	```
46	
47	### File A: `.archon/proof-journal/sessions/session_<N>/summary.md`
48	
49	Must include:
50	- Session metadata (number, sorry count before/after, targets attempted)
51	- For EACH target attempted:
52	  - **Every significant attempt** with: tactic/code tried, Lean error received, goal state at that point
53	  - What was learned from each failed attempt
54	  - For solved targets: the final proof structure with key lemmas
55	- Key findings / proof patterns discovered
56	- Recommendations for next session
57	
58	### File B: `.archon/proof-journal/sessions/session_<N>/milestones.jsonl`
59	
60	Each line MUST follow this JSON format — one entry per target theorem:
61	
62	```json
63	{
64	  "timestamp": "ISO-8601",
65	  "status": "solved|partial|blocked|not_started",
66	  "target": {
67	    "file": "path/to/File.lean",
68	    "theorem": "theorem_name"
69	  },
70	  "session": {
71	    "id": "session_N",
72	    "model": "model-name"
73	  },
74	  "findings": {
75	    "blocker": "description if blocked",
76	    "key_lemmas_used": ["lemma1", "lemma2"]
77	  },
78	  "attempts": [
79	    {
80	      "attempt": 1,
81	      "strategy": "what was tried",
82	      "code_tried": "actual Lean code or tactic",
83	      "line_number": "line number in the initial file where this code was attempted",
84	      "lean_error": "actual error message if failed",
85	      "goal_before": "the goal state before this attempt",
86	      "goal_after": "the goal state after this attempt",
87	      "result": "success|failed|partial",
88	      "insight": "what was learned from this attempt"
89	    }
90	  ],
91	  "next_steps": "..."
92	}
93	```
94	
95	**CRITICAL**: The `attempts` array must reflect ACTUAL attempts from the pre-processed data:
96	- If `attempts_raw.jsonl` shows 5 edits to a file, there should be multiple attempts recorded
97	- Each attempt must include `code_tried` (from edit events) and `lean_error` (from diagnostic events)
98	- Do NOT summarize multiple attempts as "tried various approaches" — list each one
99	
100	### File C: `.archon/proof-journal/sessions/session_<N>/recommendations.md`
101	
102	Write concrete recommendations for the next plan agent iteration:
103	- Which targets are closest to completion and should be prioritized
104	- Which approaches showed promise but need more work
105	- Which targets are blocked and why (the plan agent should NOT assign these)
106	- Any reusable proof patterns discovered
107	
108	If your analysis shows the prover has hit the exact same blocker for several consecutive iterations on the same target, you should explicitly instruct the Plan Agent to avoid retrying the same approach without putting more effort into understanding the underlying issue.
109	
110	## Step 5: Update PROJECT_STATUS.md (Knowledge Base only)
111	
112	The per-session narrative (Overall Progress: total sorry, branches closed, solved/partial/blocked/untouched, this session's analysis) goes to `iter/iter-NNN/review.md` — see the next subsection. PROJECT_STATUS.md carries only the cumulative Knowledge Base:
113	
114	```markdown
115	# Project Status
116	
117	## Knowledge Base
118	### Proof Patterns (reusable across targets)
119	- <pattern name>: <description + key lemmas>
120	
121	### Known Blockers (do not retry)
122	- <target>: <reason>
123	
124	## Last Updated
125	<ISO timestamp>
126	```
127	
128	If an existing PROJECT_STATUS.md still carries an "Overall Progress" section from a prior project that grew it, leave the old content where it is (do not delete history) but stop appending to it. New session narrative goes to `iter/iter-NNN/review.md`.
129	
130	### Per-iteration sidecars (split write)
131	
132	Your invocation prompt contains a `## Per-iteration sidecars` block that names `iter/iter-NNN/review.md` as the destination for THIS session's narrative. Split your writes accordingly:
133	
134	- **The per-session "Overall Progress" narrative goes to `iter/iter-NNN/review.md`**, NOT to PROJECT_STATUS.md's "Overall Progress" section. PROJECT_STATUS.md no longer carries a session log.
135	- **You DO keep updating PROJECT_STATUS.md's "Knowledge Base" section.** Cumulative non-obvious facts (errors not to reproduce, reusable proof patterns, Mathlib idioms that worked) still belong in the Knowledge Base. The Knowledge Base is the only growing-but-curated part of PROJECT_STATUS.md.
136	- **Format for `iter/iter-NNN/review.md`**: same fields as the Overall Progress block above (Total sorry, branches closed, solved/partial/blocked/untouched, plus any narrative analysis). Born-bounded — this file contains THIS session only, never a multi-session log.
137	- Older `iter/iter-MMM/review.md` files are on disk for you to read on demand, but the recent window is already injected in your prompt.
138	
139	## Step 6: Blueprint Markers
140	
141	`\leanok` placement is now handled deterministically by Archon's `sync_leanok` phase, which runs between the prover and you. It walks every chapter, looks up each `\lean{...}` declaration in the Lean source, runs `sorry_analyzer` + `lake env lean`, and adds/removes `\leanok` accordingly. **Do not touch `\leanok` markers yourself.** If you see one missing where you expect it, the underlying file probably doesn't compile or still has a sorry — do not paper over that with a manual edit.
142	
143	Your remaining marker responsibilities are the ones that require semantic judgement:
144	
145	- **`\mathlibok`** (statement-block only) — declaration is backed by Mathlib (re-export / alias / direct reference). The deterministic script never adds or removes this; you decide based on the prover's task result.
146	- **`\lean{...}` macro maintenance** — if a prover renamed a declaration or chose a different name from the plan agent's hint, their task result will mention it. Update the `\lean{...}` line in the chapter to the correct Lean name. If a declaration was moved to a different file by a refactor, update the chapter's location references as needed.
147	- **`% NOTE: <reason>` annotations** — when a block is unformalized because the informal statement did not translate cleanly, add a `% NOTE: ...` comment explaining the obstacle so the plan agent sees it.
148	- **Stripping `\notready`** — if a `\notready` marker still sits on a block that the prover has now landed, remove it. The deterministic script does not manage `\notready`.
149	
150	### `\mathlibok` rules (your domain)
151	
152	Add `\mathlibok` inside the statement block when:
153	- The Lean side references a Mathlib name directly (`def foo := Mathlib.bar`, `theorem foo := Mathlib.bar`, or a simple `export Mathlib.Foo (bar)`), AND
154	- The Archon-side declaration itself contains no `sorry` and introduces no new proof obligation.
155	
156	If you add `\mathlibok`, no `\leanok` is needed on the proof block — the deterministic script will leave the proof block alone if there's no Lean proof body to verify.
157	
158	### Record what you changed
159	
160	In `session_<N>/summary.md`, include a "Blueprint markers updated" section listing **only the changes you personally made** (the deterministic script's `\leanok` adds/removes are committed separately as `archon[NNN/marker-sync]` and don't need to appear here):
161	
162	```markdown
163	## Blueprint markers updated (manual)
164	- `Algebra_WLocal.tex`, `lem:finite_closed`: added `\mathlibok` (backed by `Set.Finite.isClosed`)
165	- `Core.tex`, `thm:stacks_0A31`: added `% NOTE: prover reported translation gap, see task_results/Core.md`
166	- `Core.tex`, `thm:old_name`: stripped stale `\notready`
167	- `Core.tex`, `thm:foo`: corrected `\lean{Old.foo}` → `\lean{New.foo}` after refactor rename
168	```
169	
170	### File D: `.archon/TO_USER.md`
171	
172	While the agents should be autonomous, you might want to inform the user of any critical issues that require their attention. The content of `TO_USER.md` will be surfaced in the UI as an alert banner, however the user might not see it immediately or never see it at all (its content will be refreshed every iteration before the review agent is called). It might include for instance issues with the environment, critical missing dependencies, required user actions, etc. 
173	
174	**Always check the planner's iter sidecar for escalations.** Read `.archon/iter/iter-NNN/plan.md` (this iter's plan-agent narrative). If the planner intentionally skipped provers — signaled by the `(no prover dispatch this iter ...)` marker in `PROGRESS.md`'s `## Current Objectives` section, by a "user escalation" / "hard gate fired" notice in the sidecar, or by the planner's `planValidate.status: ok_intentional_skip` meta — you MUST write a concise alert banner to `TO_USER.md` naming:
175	- What the impasse is (one sentence).
176	- What options the planner has surfaced (one bullet per option, if applicable).
177	- Where to find full context (the sidecar path).
178	
179	The planner does NOT write to `USER_HINTS.md` for escalations — `USER_HINTS.md` is user-to-planner only. The escalation banner is your responsibility; you are the user-facing channel.
180	
181	**Rules:**
182	- Be extremely concise (1-2 sentences per item, listed in markdown format).
183	- If nothing relevant for the user is detected, leave the file completely empty.
184	
185	## Step 7: Dispatch review subagents
186	
187	You may dispatch read-only review subagents BEFORE writing your consolidated summary. They are audits whose findings you incorporate into `summary.md` / `recommendations.md`. Each runs as its own fresh-context Claude process; spawning two or more in one assistant message runs them in parallel, subject to the global `max_parallel` cap.
188	
189	### Where the catalog comes from
190	
191	Your invocation prompt contains an auto-generated **Available subagents** section listing every enabled descriptor. Reviewers are typically marked `[read-only]`. A descriptor tagged `[MANDATORY]` for this phase MUST be dispatched before the phase completes. Do not `ls .archon/subagents/` — the catalog you were handed is authoritative.
192	
193	When you decide to invoke a specific reviewer, read its full prompt and directive shape from `.archon/subagents/<name>.md`.
194	
195	### How to dispatch
196	
197	Pattern (Bash tool, parallel-able, run **in foreground** so the report lands before you write your summary):
198	
199	```
200	python3 .claude/tools/archon-subagent.py \
201	  --name <subagent-name> \
202	  --slug <kebab-case-slug> \
203	  --directive-file .archon/logs/iter-NNN/<name>-<slug>-directive.md \
204	  --write-domain 'task_results/**'
205	```
206	
207	Each directive must be **fully self-contained** — the reviewer reads only what the directive points it at. The directive shape for each reviewer is documented in its descriptor under `.archon/subagents/<name>.md`.
208	
209	### When NOT to dispatch
210	
211	- If this session was a pure proof-filling round with no new definitions or refactors, skip reviewers. They add latency and cost for no value.
212	- If a previous session in the last 3 iters already ran the same audit on the same scope, don't repeat unless that scope changed.
213	- The plan agent may also have dispatched reviewers proactively — check `task_results/` for matching reports before doubling up.
214	
215	### Incorporating findings
216	
217	After the reviewers return, read every report. Land each finding in your `summary.md` and `recommendations.md`:
218	
219	- **CRITICAL / HIGH** findings → bullet at the top of `recommendations.md` with the suggested action.
220	- **MEDIUM** findings → bullet in `recommendations.md`'s body.
221	- **LOW** findings → one-liner in `summary.md`'s notes section.
222	
223	Do NOT repeat the full report content in your summary — link to the report file. The plan agent reads your summary, not the raw reports.
224	
225	## Step 8: Self-Validation
226	
227	After writing all files, validate your output by checking:
228	- [ ] milestones.jsonl has valid JSON on every line
229	- [ ] Each milestone has `target.file`, `target.theorem`, `status`
230	- [ ] Each non-blocked milestone has at least 1 attempt with `code_tried` or `strategy`
231	- [ ] Number of attempts per milestone is proportional to edits in `attempts_raw.jsonl`
232	- [ ] summary.md includes specific code/errors, not just high-level summaries
233	- [ ] recommendations.md includes actionable next steps
234	- [ ] You did NOT add or remove any `\leanok` marker yourself (those are handled by the deterministic `sync_leanok` phase that ran before you)
235	- [ ] For every Mathlib-backed declaration the prover reported, the blueprint chapter has `\mathlibok` (your domain)
236	- [ ] Any `\lean{...}` macro rename flagged in a `task_results/<file>.md` has been applied
237	- [ ] No `\notready` marker remains on a block whose Lean declaration now exists
238	
239	## Permissions

241	You may write to:
242	- `.archon/proof-journal/sessions/session_<N>/` (summary.md, milestones.jsonl, recommendations.md)
243	- `.archon/PROJECT_STATUS.md`
244	- `blueprint/src/chapters/*.tex` — markers (`\leanok`, `\mathlibok`), `\lean{...}` macro corrections, stale-marker cleanup, and `% NOTE:` comments. Do NOT rewrite the informal prose — that is the plan agent's surface.

249	- `.archon/task_pending.md` or `.archon/task_done.md` (plan agent's responsibility)
250	- Blueprint informal content (theorem/proof prose, chapter structure) — only the markers and `% NOTE:` comments are yours.

5	**Starting state**: 4 sorries (L109 algebra-instance letI, L112 `appLE_isLocalization`
6	body, L142 module-instance letI, L145 bridge body).
7	
8	**Ending state**: 1 sorry (the main `appLE_isLocalization` body, L282) — down from 4.
9	
10	**Net change**: **−3 sorries**, +6 fully proved declarations (helpers + factorisation +
11	bridge body + Step 0 unit-transport lemma + M1.c/M1.d).
12	
13	File compiles cleanly (only the documented `sorry` warning at L282).
14	
15	## Resolved sorries
16	
17	### L109 — `Algebra Γ(S, U) A_colim` letI (RESOLVED, via `archon-lean4:lean4-sorry-filler-deep`)
18	Replaced by a `noncomputable def appLE_colimAlgebra` built from the new helper
19	`appLE_colimRingHom`. The instance is now provided by a top-level reducible def,
20	not an inline `sorry`-ed `letI`.
21	
22	### L142 — `Module Γ(X, V)` letI on the presheaf section (RESOLVED)
23	Replaced by `inferInstanceAs (Module Γ(X, V) (((relativeDifferentialsPresheaf f).obj (.op V)) : Type _))` —
24	the PresheafOfModules structure provides the module instance.
25	
26	### L145 — bridge body M1.e (RESOLVED, modulo M1.b)
27	The body of `relativeDifferentialsPresheaf_equiv_kaehler_appLE` is fully closed via
28	`(kaehler_quotient_localization_iso M).symm` — leveraging the `IsScalarTower` from
29	`IsScalarTower.of_algebraMap_eq'` plus the new `appLE_colimRingHom_comp_φV`
30	factorisation. The proof goes through *as soon as* `appLE_isLocalization` is closed.
31	
32	### L164 — `isUnit_appLE_unitSubmonoid_in_colim` Step 0 lemma (RESOLVED, this session)
33	**Strategy**: For `g ∈ M` (so `(appLE f).hom g` is a unit in `Γ(X, V)`):
34	1. From `Scheme.basicOpen_of_isUnit` + `Scheme.basicOpen_appLE`, derive
35	   `V ≤ f ⁻¹ᵁ (S.basicOpen g)`.
36	2. Build the cocone leg `cleg : Γ(S, S.basicOpen g) ⟶ A_colim` (unit of the
37	   pullback/pushforward adjunction at `op (S.basicOpen g)` composed with the
38	   restriction along `(homOfLE hVle).op`).
39	3. Prove `hcompat : appLE_colimRingHom f e = rstr ≫ cleg` where `rstr` is the
40	   restriction `Γ(S, U) → Γ(S, S.basicOpen g)`. This is a naturality + functoriality
41	   argument: factor `homOfLE e = homOfLE hVle ≫ homOfLE hpreLe` (with
42	   `hpreLe : f ⁻¹ᵁ S.basicOpen g ≤ f ⁻¹ᵁ U`), use `Functor.map_comp` to split
43	   `Pf.map (homOfLE e).op` into a composition, then apply naturality of the
44	   adjunction unit at `homOfLE (S.basicOpen_le g).op`.
45	4. By `IsAffineOpen.isLocalization_basicOpen hU g`, `Γ(S, S.basicOpen g)` is
46	   `IsLocalization.Away g`, so `algebraMap g` is a unit there
47	   (`IsLocalization.Away.algebraMap_isUnit`).
48	5. Push through `cleg` (ring homs preserve units) to conclude `g` is a unit
49	   in `A_colim`.
50	
51	**Total LOC**: ~70 lines.
52	
53	## Helper lemmas (added by deep prover, fully proved)
54	
55	- `appLE_colimRingHom` (def, L97) — canonical map `Γ(S, U) → A_colim`.
56	- `appLE_colimAlgebra` (reducible def, L106) — the algebra structure from
57	  `(appLE_colimRingHom f e).hom.toAlgebra`.
58	- `appLE_colimRingHom_comp_φV` (theorem, L116) — factorisation
59	  `appLE_colimRingHom ≫ φV = appLE`, the cocone-leg triangle.
60	- `kaehler_localization_subsingleton` (theorem) — M1.c, the subsingleton
61	  conclusion via `Algebra.FormallyUnramified.of_isLocalization`.
62	- `kaehler_quotient_localization_iso` (def) — M1.d, tower-cancellation
63	  `Ω[B/A] ≃ₗ[B] Ω[B/L]` (the most extractable Mathlib-contribution candidate;
64	  candidate name `KaehlerDifferential.equivOfFormallyUnramified`).
65	
66	## Remaining sorry
67	
68	### L282 — `appLE_isLocalization` body (the main M1.b)
69	The full four-step `IsLocalization.of_le` construction:
70	- Step 0 (`isUnit_appLE_unitSubmonoid_in_colim`): **DONE** ✓
71	- Step 1: `A_M → A_colim` via `IsLocalization.lift` (using Step 0). **TODO**
72	- Step 2: `A_colim → A_M` via the colimit universal property + basic-open
73	  refinements (each `Γ(S, W) → A_M` for `f V ⊆ W ⊆ U` factors through some
74	  `Γ(S, D(g)) = (Γ(S, U))_g` via quasi-compactness of `f V`). **TODO**
75	- Step 3: Composites are identities (`IsLocalization.ringHom_ext` +
76	  `IsColimit.hom_ext` on the colimit cocone). **TODO**
77	- Step 4: `IsLocalization.isLocalization_of_algEquiv` from the resulting
78	  `Localization M ≃ₐ[Γ(S, U)] A_colim`. **TODO**
79	
80	**Estimated remaining LOC**: 100-250 (originally estimated 200-400 for the
81	whole M1.b, of which Step 0 alone took ~70 LOC — so the remaining four steps
82	are estimated at 100-250 LOC).
83	
84	**Next-session next step**: build the `Localization M → A_colim` map first
85	(Step 1, easiest; uses the just-closed Step 0). Then tackle Step 2 (hardest).
86	
87	## Sorry trajectory
88	
89	| Stage | sorries in file |
90	|---|---|
91	| Before iter-122 prover lane | 4 |
92	| After deep-prover subagent | 2 (L164 Step 0 + L282 M1.b body) |
93	| After this session | **1** (L282 M1.b body) |
94	
95	**Project total**: 5 → 2 (Differentials.lean: 1, Jacobian.lean: 1 unchanged).
96	
97	## Mathlib API discovered/confirmed
98	
99	- `Scheme.basicOpen_appLE` (in `Mathlib.AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme`):
100	  `X.basicOpen ((f.appLE V U e).hom s) = U ⊓ f ⁻¹ᵁ (Y.basicOpen s)`.
101	- `Scheme.basicOpen_of_isUnit`: `IsUnit f → X.basicOpen f = U`.
102	- `Scheme.basicOpen_le`: `X.basicOpen f ≤ U`.
103	- `IsAffineOpen.isLocalization_basicOpen`: produces `IsLocalization.Away f` for
104	  the basic-open-section algebra.
105	- `Scheme.algebra_section_section_basicOpen` (instance): the canonical `Algebra`
106	  structure on the basic-open section ring, which makes the above predicate
107	  type-check.
108	- `IsLocalization.Away.algebraMap_isUnit`: the element is a unit under the
109	  algebra map.
110	
111	## Technical lessons (this session)
112	
113	1. **`rw [Functor.map_comp]` fails on `((TopCat.Presheaf.pullback _ _).obj _).map (f ≫ g)`**
114	   despite the pattern being literally present. Likely due to category-instance
115	   metadata mismatch under the `Lan`-defined functor. Workaround: pre-prove
116	   `hmc : self.map (f ≫ g) = self.map f ≫ self.map g` as a `have` via
117	   `Functor.map_comp _ _ _`, then use `erw [hmc]` (`erw` succeeds where `rw`
118	   fails for these unification edge-cases).
119	2. **`rw [Category.assoc]` similarly fails** under category instance metadata
120	   noise. Workaround: use `exact Category.assoc _ _ _` directly when the goal
121	   is exactly the associativity equation.
122	3. **`show`/`change` on `algebraMap` doesn't work** even when the algebra
123	   instance is `appLE_colimAlgebra := φ.hom.toAlgebra` (which should make
124	   `algebraMap = φ.hom` definitionally). Workaround: route the conclusion
125	   through `IsUnit ((appLE_colimRingHom f e).hom g)` first via a `have h_factor`,
126	   then `exact` the result — Lean's elaboration of `IsUnit` and the algebra
127	   instance aligns when the term is `exact`-ed rather than `change`-d.
128	4. **`adj.unit.naturality` produces an equation involving the identity functor**
129	   `((𝟭 _).obj _)`; use `simpa using` to clean it up.
130	
131	## Status
132	
133	**PARTIAL** — meeting and exceeding the iter-122 plan's "PARTIAL is acceptable"
134	target. The most surprising achievement is closing the M1.e bridge body (L145)
135	modulo M1.b, which the plan agent had labelled "secondary, lower priority"; this
136	was made possible by the Step 0 closure giving the bridge a `localization`
137	hypothesis to use.
138	
139	The remaining sorry is the M1.b core (Steps 1-4), which requires presheaf-level
140	cofinality machinery and is estimated at 100-250 LOC. Ready for an iter-123
141	prover lane.
142	
143	## Blueprint marker recommendations (for review agent)
144	
145	- `lem:appLE_isLocalization` (L153 in `Differentials.tex`) — still has 1 sorry,
146	  do NOT mark `\leanok` on the proof; the **statement** is formalized (the
147	  theorem signature is in place), so `\leanok` on the statement is correct.
148	- `def:appLE_unitSubmonoid` (not in blueprint yet? — has a fully-proved def
149	  but the blueprint lemma title is `lem:appLE_isLocalization`). The
150	  `appLE_unitSubmonoid` def is the M itself, which the blueprint introduces in
151	  M1.a paragraph at L130-135 (no separate lemma label). No marker change.
152	- `lem:kaehler_localization_subsingleton` (L188) — **fully proved**, mark `\leanok`
153	  on both statement and proof.
154	- `lem:kaehler_quotient_localization_iso` (L199) — **fully proved**, mark `\leanok`
155	  on both statement and proof.
156	- `thm:relativeDifferentialsPresheaf_equiv_kaehler_appLE` (L113) — fully proved
157	  *modulo* `appLE_isLocalization` (which is invoked as a hypothesis), so the
158	  proof is technically not yet `\leanok`-able (depends on a sorry). Statement
159	  is `\leanok`-able. Project policy: `sync_leanok` will detect the transitive
160	  sorry and leave `\leanok` off the proof — this is correct.
161	
162	## Files changed
163	
164	- `AlgebraicJacobian/Differentials.lean` (only file modified).
165

# Iter-122 (Archon canonical) plan-agent run

## Headline outcome (DRAFT — finalised after critics return)

Iter-121 was a plan-phase-only strategic-pivot iter under a user
directive, with both `Differentials.tex` and `Jacobian.tex` returning
`complete: partial` (HARD GATE deferral of the M1 prover lane). The
iter-121 blueprint-writers landed expanded prose for both chapters; the
iter-121 mathlib-analogist-bridge returned 5 ALIGN_WITH_MATHLIB + 1
NEEDS_MATHLIB_GAP_FILL with REDESIGNED approach (the bridge API shape
recommendations + the M1.b re-framing via `IsLocalization.of_le`).

Iter-122 plan phase:

1. **Archived iter-121 task_results** (`lean-auditor-review121`,
   `lean-vs-blueprint-checker-{differentials,jacobian}-review121`)
   to `logs/iter-121/` and cleared from `task_results/`.

2. **Applied inline blueprint corrections** to incorporate the iter-121
   mathlib-analogist findings + lean-vs-blueprint-checker-review121
   findings:
   - `Differentials.tex`:
     - Bridge theorem renamed: `_iso_` → `_equiv_`
       (`thm:relativeDifferentialsPresheaf_equiv_kaehler_appLE`,
       and `\lean{...}` hint updated to match).
     - Bridge statement rephrased to "$B$-linear equivalence"
       (matching `LinearEquiv` packaging per analogist).
     - Auxiliary lemma `appLE_isLocalization` namespace fix:
       `Scheme.appLE_isLocalization` →
       `IsAffineOpen.appLE_isLocalization`.
     - M1.b proof body completely rewritten: the `Functor.Final`
       cofinality framing is replaced with the two-direction
       `IsLocalization.of_le` construction (Steps 0–4, using
       `IsLocalization.lift` for `A_M → A_colim`, cocone universal
       property for `A_colim → A_M`, `IsLocalization.ringHom_ext`
       for composite-identity verification). Per analogist this is
       the canonical pattern; Mathlib has no off-the-shelf "colim
       of localizations = localization at union submonoid" lemma.
     - M1.c framing corrected: previously labelled a Mathlib gap;
       now correctly stated as a thin re-export of
       `Algebra.FormallyUnramified.of_isLocalization` +
       `subsingleton_kaehlerDifferential` instance (the previously-
       cited "no off-the-shelf bare lemma" claim was wrong).
     - M1.d body updated to use the corrected Mathlib closure pieces
       (`KaehlerDifferential.exact_mapBaseChange_map` +
       `map_surjective` via `LinearEquiv.ofBijective`).
     - Mathlib contribution candidate (Remark `rem:bridge_mathlib_pr`)
       updated: lemma `kaehler_quotient_localization_iso` is now
       the primary Mathlib contribution candidate (not
       `appLE_isLocalization`), per the analogist's analysis.
     - The `lem:kaehler_quotient_localization_iso` proof body
       rewritten to use the analogist's recommended `LinearEquiv`
       construction via `LinearEquiv.ofBijective` on
       `KaehlerDifferential.map`.

   - `Jacobian.tex`:
     - Line 376 `(γ)`-bullet rewritten: removed the stale
       `Hom(P¹_k, A) = A(k)` + `C ≅ ℙ¹_k`-with-rational-point
       framing; replaced with rigidity over `k̄` + Galois descent
       framing matching the iter-121 C.2 rewrite.
     - Line 388 (Layer I direct definition): same stale framing
       replaced with the new base-change-and-descent framing.

3. **Dispatched the three mandatory critics** in parallel:
   - `strategy-critic-iter122` — re-verification (strategy unchanged
     since iter-121 rewrite; the iter-121 challenges were absorbed
     into the rewrite).
   - `blueprint-reviewer-iter122` — confirms HARD GATE clearance on
     Differentials.tex and Jacobian.tex.
   - `progress-critic-iter122` — assesses the M1 route's iter-117
     to iter-121 signals (M2/M3 are blocked behind M1; not active
     this iter).

   See § "Critic verdicts" below.

## Critic verdicts

### strategy-critic-iter122 → CHALLENGE (M1, M2, M3); 2 alternatives raised

The iter-122 strategy-critic returned CHALLENGE on all three milestones

1	# Project Progress

15	/-! # Relative Kähler differentials for schemes (presheaf form)
16	
17	This file constructs the **presheaf** of relative Kähler differentials
18	`Ω_{X/S}` of a morphism of schemes `f : X → S` and states the
19	characterisation of smoothness in terms of pointwise local freeness of
20	`Ω` on affine opens.
21	
22	The construction builds on Mathlib's ring-theoretic `KaehlerDifferential`
23	and the presheaf-of-modules differential construction
24	`PresheafOfModules.DifferentialsConstruction.relativeDifferentials'`.
25	
26	## References
27	
28	Blueprint: `blueprint/src/chapters/Differentials.tex`.
29	-/
30	
31	set_option autoImplicit false
32	
33	universe u
34	
35	open CategoryTheory Limits TopologicalSpace AlgebraicGeometry
36	
37	namespace AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme
38	
39	variable {X S : Scheme.{u}}
40	
41	/-- The relative cotangent **presheaf** of a morphism of schemes `f : X ⟶ S`.
42	
43	On each open `U ⊆ X`, the sections are the Kähler differential module of
44	the ring map `O_S(f(U)) → O_X(U)` induced by `f`. More precisely, we use
45	the inverse-image presheaf `f⁻¹ O_S` on `X` and the canonical map to `O_X`;
46	the Kähler differential construction then gives a presheaf of `O_X`-modules.
47	
48	Mathlib leverage: `TopCat.Presheaf.pullback` for `f⁻¹`,
49	`PresheafOfModules.DifferentialsConstruction.relativeDifferentials'`
50	for the presheaf of Kähler differentials. -/
51	noncomputable def relativeDifferentialsPresheaf (f : X ⟶ S) : X.PresheafOfModules :=
52	  let φ' := ((TopCat.Presheaf.pullbackPushforwardAdjunction CommRingCat f.base).homEquiv _ _).symm
53	    f.c
54	  PresheafOfModules.DifferentialsConstruction.relativeDifferentials' φ'
55	
56	/-- Localisation-compatibility identity: on each open `V ⊆ X`, the sections
57	of `relativeDifferentialsPresheaf f` over `V` are *definitionally* the
58	Kähler differential module of the ring map
59	`(f⁻¹ O_S)(V) → O_X(V)`. -/
60	theorem relativeDifferentialsPresheaf_obj_kaehler (f : X ⟶ S)
61	    (V : (TopologicalSpace.Opens X.toTopCat)ᵒᵖ) :
62	    ((relativeDifferentialsPresheaf f).presheaf.obj V : Type _) =
63	      CommRingCat.KaehlerDifferential
64	        (((TopCat.Presheaf.pullbackPushforwardAdjunction CommRingCat
65	          f.base).homEquiv _ _).symm f.c |>.app V) :=
66	  rfl
67	
68	end AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme
69	
70	namespace AlgebraicGeometry.IsAffineOpen
71	
72	variable {X S : Scheme.{u}}
73	
74	/-- The submonoid of "good" elements in `Γ(S, U)`: those whose image under the
75	appLE algebra map `Γ(S, U) → Γ(X, V)` induced by `f : X ⟶ S` is a unit in
76	`Γ(X, V)`. This is the multiplicative set at which `Γ(S, U) → A_colim` is a
77	localization (cf. `appLE_isLocalization`). -/
78	def appLE_unitSubmonoid (f : X ⟶ S)
79	    {U : S.Opens} {V : X.Opens} (_hU : IsAffineOpen U) (_hV : IsAffineOpen V)
80	    (e : V ≤ f ⁻¹ᵁ U) : Submonoid Γ(S, U) where
81	  carrier := { g | IsUnit ((Scheme.Hom.appLE f U V e).hom g) }
82	  one_mem' := by
83	    simp only [Set.mem_setOf_eq, map_one]
84	    exact isUnit_one
85	  mul_mem' := by
86	    intro a b ha hb
87	    simp only [Set.mem_setOf_eq, map_mul] at ha hb ⊢
88	    exact ha.mul hb
89	
90	/-- The canonical ring map `Γ(S, U) → A_colim` where
91	`A_colim = ((TopCat.Presheaf.pullback CommRingCat f.base).obj S.presheaf).obj (.op V)`
92	is the inverse-image-presheaf colimit ring at `V`.
93	
94	It is built as the composition of the cocone leg at `op U`
95	(the unit of the pullback/pushforward adjunction) with the restriction
96	along `(homOfLE e).op : op (f ⁻¹ᵁ U) ⟶ op V`. -/
97	noncomputable def appLE_colimRingHom (f : X ⟶ S)
98	    {U : S.Opens} {V : X.Opens} (e : V ≤ f ⁻¹ᵁ U) :
99	    Γ(S, U) ⟶ (((TopCat.Presheaf.pullback CommRingCat f.base).obj S.presheaf).obj (.op V)) :=
100	  ((TopCat.Presheaf.pullbackPushforwardAdjunction CommRingCat f.base).unit.app
101	        S.presheaf).app (.op U) ≫
102	    ((TopCat.Presheaf.pullback CommRingCat f.base).obj S.presheaf).map (homOfLE e).op
103	
104	/-- The `Algebra Γ(S, U) A_colim` structure transported from `appLE_colimRingHom f e`. -/
105	@[reducible]
106	noncomputable def appLE_colimAlgebra (f : X ⟶ S)
107	    {U : S.Opens} {V : X.Opens} (e : V ≤ f ⁻¹ᵁ U) :
108	    Algebra Γ(S, U)
109	      (((TopCat.Presheaf.pullback CommRingCat f.base).obj S.presheaf).obj (.op V)) :=
110	  (appLE_colimRingHom f e).hom.toAlgebra
111	
112	/-- **Factorisation of the `appLE` ring map through the colimit ring.**
113	The composition `Γ(S, U) → A_colim → Γ(X, V)` (the colim algebra-map composed
114	with the restriction `φ' := (homEquiv).symm f.c` at `op V`) equals
115	`Scheme.Hom.appLE f U V e`. This is the cocone-leg triangle identity. -/
116	theorem appLE_colimRingHom_comp_φV (f : X ⟶ S)
117	    {U : S.Opens} {V : X.Opens} (e : V ≤ f ⁻¹ᵁ U) :
118	    appLE_colimRingHom f e ≫
119	        (((TopCat.Presheaf.pullbackPushforwardAdjunction CommRingCat f.base).homEquiv _ _).symm
120	          f.c).app (.op V) =

270	of `Γ(S, U)` at the submonoid `appLE_unitSubmonoid f hU hV e`.
271	
272	This is milestone M1.b of the project roadmap: the heart of the bridge between
273	the section module of `relativeDifferentialsPresheaf` and the appLE-algebra
274	Kähler module on an affine chart. The proof goes via the two-direction
275	`IsLocalization.of_le` pattern (see
276	`blueprint/src/chapters/Differentials.tex § sec:bridge`).
277	
278	The canonical `Algebra Γ(S, U) A_colim` structure is the cocone leg of the
279	directed colimit at the open `U` (valid since `V ≤ f ⁻¹ᵁ U` means
280	`fV ⊆ U`, so `U` is in the index category of the colimit). It is
281	constructed in `appLE_colimAlgebra`. -/
282	theorem appLE_isLocalization (f : X ⟶ S)
283	    {U : S.Opens} {V : X.Opens} (hU : IsAffineOpen U) (hV : IsAffineOpen V)
284	    (e : V ≤ f ⁻¹ᵁ U) :
285	    letI : Algebra Γ(S, U)
286	        (((TopCat.Presheaf.pullback CommRingCat f.base).obj S.presheaf).obj (.op V)) :=

288	    IsLocalization (appLE_unitSubmonoid f hU hV e)
289	      (((TopCat.Presheaf.pullback CommRingCat f.base).obj S.presheaf).obj (.op V)) := by
290	  -- Strategy from blueprint § sec:bridge (M1.b proof):
291	  -- Step 0 (`isUnit_appLE_unitSubmonoid_in_colim`): each `g ∈ M` is a unit in

293	  -- Step 1: `IsLocalization.lift` produces `A_M → A_colim`.
294	  -- Step 2: cocone universal property + basic-open refinement gives

296	  --   `Γ(S, D(g)) = (Γ(S, U))_g` for some `g ∈ M`, via quasi-compactness of
297	  --   `f V` and a basic-open cover).
298	  -- Step 3: Composites are identities via `IsLocalization.ringHom_ext` and
299	  --   `IsColimit.hom_ext` on the colimit cocone.
300	  -- Step 4: Conclude via `IsLocalization.isLocalization_of_algEquiv` applied
301	  --   to the resulting `(Localization M) ≃ₐ[Γ(S, U)] A_colim`.
302	  -- The full proof is estimated at 200-400 LOC of presheaf-level cofinality
303	  -- machinery (see `blueprint/src/chapters/Differentials.tex § sec:bridge`).
304	  sorry
305	
306	end AlgebraicGeometry.IsAffineOpen
307	
308	namespace AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme
309	
310	variable {X S : Scheme.{u}}
311	
312	/-- The Kähler module of a localization is subsingleton.
313	This is project-local Lemma `lem:kaehler_localization_subsingleton`. -/
314	theorem kaehler_localization_subsingleton
315	    {A L : Type u} [CommRing A] [CommRing L] [Algebra A L]
316	    (M : Submonoid A) [IsLocalization M L] :
317	    Subsingleton (Ω[L⁄A]) :=
318	  letI : Algebra.FormallyUnramified A L :=
319	    Algebra.FormallyUnramified.of_isLocalization (R := A) (Rₘ := L) M
320	  Algebra.FormallyUnramified.subsingleton_kaehlerDifferential
321	
322	/-- Tower-cancellation `LinearEquiv` for the Kähler module under a localization:
323	if `A → L` is a localization at `M ⊆ A` and `L → B` is a ring map forming a
324	scalar tower `A → L → B`, then the canonical map `Ω[B/A] → Ω[B/L]` is a
325	`B`-linear equivalence.
326	
327	This is project-local Lemma `lem:kaehler_quotient_localization_iso` and the most
328	extractable Mathlib contribution candidate of milestone M1 (candidate name
329	`KaehlerDifferential.equivOfFormallyUnramified`). -/
330	noncomputable def kaehler_quotient_localization_iso
331	    {A L B : Type u} [CommRing A] [CommRing L] [CommRing B]
332	    [Algebra A L] [Algebra A B] [Algebra L B] [IsScalarTower A L B]
333	    (M : Submonoid A) [IsLocalization M L] :
334	    Ω[B⁄A] ≃ₗ[B] Ω[B⁄L] := by
335	  haveI : Subsingleton (Ω[L⁄A]) := kaehler_localization_subsingleton M
336	  refine LinearEquiv.ofBijective (KaehlerDifferential.map A L B B) ⟨?_, ?_⟩
337	  · rw [injective_iff_map_eq_zero]
338	    intro x hx
339	    obtain ⟨y, rfl⟩ :=
340	      (KaehlerDifferential.exact_mapBaseChange_map A L B x).mp hx
341	    -- y : B ⊗[L] Ω[L⁄A]; since Ω[L⁄A] is subsingleton, the tensor product is too
342	    haveI : Subsingleton (TensorProduct L B (Ω[L⁄A])) := by
343	      refine ⟨fun a b => ?_⟩
344	      have hzero : ∀ z : TensorProduct L B (Ω[L⁄A]), z = 0 := by
345	        intro z
346	        induction z with
347	        | zero => rfl
348	        | tmul x y =>
349	          rw [show y = (0 : Ω[L⁄A]) from Subsingleton.elim _ _, TensorProduct.tmul_zero]
350	        | add x y hx hy => rw [hx, hy, add_zero]
351	      rw [hzero a, hzero b]
352	    rw [Subsingleton.elim y 0, map_zero]
353	  · exact KaehlerDifferential.map_surjective _ _ _
354	
355	/-- The bridge between the presheaf form and the algebra-Kähler form of the
356	relative cotangent module on an affine chart (milestone M1 of the project
357	roadmap). The section module of `relativeDifferentialsPresheaf f` over an
358	affine `V ⊆ f ⁻¹ᵁ U` is `Γ(X, V)`-linearly equivalent to the appLE-algebra
359	Kähler module `CommRingCat.KaehlerDifferential (Scheme.Hom.appLE f U V e)`.
360	
361	See `blueprint/src/chapters/Differentials.tex § sec:bridge` for the proof
362	strategy (M1.a–M1.e).
363	
364	The `Module Γ(X, V)` instance on the LHS section module is supplied by the
365	`PresheafOfModules` structure (via the underlying
366	`(relativeDifferentialsPresheaf f).obj (.op V) : ModuleCat _`); the
367	canonical transport-of-instance is recorded by the `letI` below as
368	`inferInstanceAs` from the underlying `ModuleCat`. -/
369	noncomputable def relativeDifferentialsPresheaf_equiv_kaehler_appLE

120	          f.c).app (.op V) =
121	      Scheme.Hom.appLE f U V e := by
122	  -- Strategy: appLE_colimRingHom = (unit at op U) ≫ (pullback restrict),
123	  -- and (pullback restrict) ≫ φV.app (op V) = φV.app (op (f⁻¹ U)) ≫
124	  -- (X.presheaf.map (homOfLE e).op) by naturality of φV; combined with the
125	  -- unit-triangle for f.c, (unit at op U) ≫ φV.app (op (f⁻¹ U)) = f.app U.

430	/-- Forward direction of the Jacobian criterion (algebra-Kähler form).
431	If `f : X → S` is smooth of relative dimension `n`, then for every
432	point `x ∈ X` there exist affine opens `U ⊆ S` and `V ⊆ X` with

437	The bridge from this algebra-Kähler form to the project's presheaf
438	form `(relativeDifferentialsPresheaf f).presheaf.obj (.op V)` is a
439	Mathlib gap: the section module of the presheaf identifies with the
440	Kähler module over the inverse-image-presheaf colimit ring
441	`colim_{f V ⊆ W ⊆ S} Γ(S, W)`, which is a localization of `Γ(S, U)`
442	and hence yields an iso `Ω[Γ(X, V) ⁄ Γ(S, U)] ≃ Ω[Γ(X, V) ⁄ A_colim]`
443	via `KaehlerDifferential.isLocalizedModule_map`; constructing the iso
444	requires presheaf-level cofinality machinery (~200–400 LOC) that is
445	out of autonomous-loop scope. See `blueprint/src/chapters/Differentials.tex`
446	§ "Bridge to the relative cotangent presheaf — out of autonomous-loop
447	scope" for the mathematical content.
448	
449	The reverse direction (locally free Ω of rank `n` implies smooth of
450	relative dimension `n`) is **mathematically false** as stated without
451	additional deformation-theoretic input (vanishing of
452	`Algebra.H1Cotangent A B` on each chart); see the chapter
453	`Differentials.tex` for the counterexample (`Spec k → Spec k[t]`,
454	`t ↦ 0`) and the converse-direction disclosure. -/

458	        x ∈ V ∧ IsAffineOpen U ∧ IsAffineOpen V ∧
459	          letI : Algebra Γ(S, U) Γ(X, V) :=
460	            (Scheme.Hom.appLE f U V e).hom.toAlgebra
461	          (Module.Free Γ(X, V) (Ω[Γ(X, V) ⁄ Γ(S, U)]) ∧
462	           Module.rank Γ(X, V) (Ω[Γ(X, V) ⁄ Γ(S, U)]) = n) := by
463	  intro x

224	### Proof Patterns (reusable across targets)
225	
226	- **`Functor.Final` is the WRONG framing for "directed colimit of localizations = localization at the union submonoid"; use `IsLocalization.of_le` with cocone universality**
227	  *(NEW iter-121 Archon canonical — surfaced by `mathlib-analogist-bridge-iter121`)*:
228	  the natural-looking colim-comparison shape
229	  ```
230	  colim_{g ∈ M, in the basic-open-cocone} A_g  ≃  Localization M A
231	  ```
232	  has no off-the-shelf Mathlib lemma in `b80f227` (verified via
233	  `lean_leansearch`, `lean_loogle`, `lean_local_search` on multiple
234	  variants). Trying to use `CategoryTheory.Functor.Final` to reduce
235	  to a cofinality-of-indexing-category argument leads into Mathlib
236	  territory that doesn't exist. The cleanly Mathlib-aligned path is
237	  to **bypass the colim-comparison entirely** and prove
238	  `IsLocalization M A_colim` directly:
239	  (a) Construct `A_M → A_colim` via `Localization M`'s universal
240	      property (after showing each `g ∈ M` is a unit in `A_colim`).
241	  (b) Construct `A_colim → A_M` via the colim cocone's universal
242	      property, picking basic-open refinements `D(g) ⊆ W` on each
243	      open and mapping `Γ(S, W) → Γ(S, D(g)) = A_g → A_M`.
244	  (c) Verify the composites are identity via
245	      `IsLocalization.ringHom_ext`.
246	  (d) Conclude `IsLocalization M A_colim` via `IsLocalization.of_le`
247	      (`Mathlib.RingTheory.Localization.Defs`:
248	      `[IsLocalization M S] → (h : ∀ n ∈ N, IsUnit (algebraMap n)) → IsLocalization N S`).
249	  Reusable for any "directed colimit of localizations = localization
250	  at union" claim in Lean. **Documented at
251	  `analogies/relative-differentials-presheaf-bridge.md` and
252	  `STRATEGY.md § Roadmap M1` (iter-121).**
253	
254	- **`KaehlerDifferential` of a localization is `Subsingleton` via `FormallyUnramified.of_isLocalization` — NOT a Mathlib gap**
255	  *(NEW iter-121 Archon canonical — surfaced by `mathlib-analogist-bridge-iter121`)*:
256	  the claim "`Ω[L⁄A]` is subsingleton when `A → L` is a localization"
257	  is NOT a Mathlib gap, contrary to the iter-121 first-draft blueprint.
258	  Mathlib supplies it as the 2-line composition:
259	  ```
260	  [IsLocalization M L]
261	    → Algebra.FormallyUnramified.of_isLocalization M
262	         (Mathlib.RingTheory.Unramified.Basic:303)
263	    → @Algebra.FormallyUnramified.subsingleton_kaehlerDifferential _ _ _ _ _ _
264	         (Mathlib.RingTheory.Unramified.Basic:57-59, instance)
265	  ```
266	  Use this whenever a proof needs `Ω[L/A] = 0` from a localization
267	  hypothesis; do NOT carry it around as a standalone `\lean{...}`-tagged
268	  declaration. The corollary "Kähler module of a quotient-localization
269	  iso" (`tensorKaehlerEquivOfFormallyEtale`-style: `Ω[B/A] ≃ₗ[B] Ω[B/L]`
270	  for `L = Localization M A`) is ~10–30 LOC via
271	  `KaehlerDifferential.exact_mapBaseChange_map` +
272	  `KaehlerDifferential.map_surjective`, and is the most extractable
273	  Mathlib contribution candidate from M1 (generalises
274	  `tensorKaehlerEquivOfFormallyEtale` to "only the lower step is
275	  formally unramified"). **Documented at
276	  `analogies/relative-differentials-presheaf-bridge.md` and
277	  `STRATEGY.md § Roadmap M1` (iter-121).**

280	  *(NEW iter-121 Archon canonical)*: dispatching `mathlib-analogist`
281	  proactively against a not-yet-shipped infrastructure declaration

284	  findings (LinearEquiv shape with `@[simps]`; bare-theorem

287	  cofinality re-framing). Each of these would have cost a refactor +

301	- **`by_cases h : genus C = 0` is a well-formed Lean decomposition for `nonempty_jacobianWitness`-style protected signatures with no genus parameter**
302	  *(NEW iter-121 Archon canonical — strategy-critic-iter121 alternative #1 adopted)*:
303	  when a protected signature quantifies over a `Nat`-genus-implicit
304	  object (here: `C : Over (Spec (.of k))` with smoothness +
305	  irreducibility + properness instances but no `g : ℕ` parameter),
306	  the body may still be a `Nat`-valued case split because
307	  `AlgebraicGeometry.genus C : ℕ` is `DecidableEq`. The shape
308	  ```
309	  theorem nonempty_jacobianWitness ... := by
310	    by_cases h : AlgebraicGeometry.genus (k := k) C.left = 0
311	    · exact ⟨genusZeroWitness C h⟩      -- closed by M2
312	    · exact ⟨positiveGenusWitness C (Nat.pos_of_ne_zero h)⟩  -- closed by M3
313	  ```

315	  Lean-level decomposition, which the autonomous loop can then close

318	  **Documented at `STRATEGY.md § Decomposition: genus-stratified
319	  body of nonempty_jacobianWitness` (iter-121).**

322	  *(NEW iter-121 Archon canonical — strategy-critic-iter121 alternative #2 adopted)*:
323	  the iter-121 first-draft strategy claimed M2.c was the

326	  `nonempty_jacobianWitness` signature, which permits curves

328	  (`x² + y² + z² = 0` is a smooth proper geometrically irreducible
329	  curve of genus 0 with no `ℚ`-point and is NOT isomorphic to

331	  `C_{k̄}` is automatically nonempty geometrically integral, hence

333	  for `ℙ¹_{k̄} → A_{k̄}`; Galois-descend constancy back to `k`.

6:The downstream role of this chapter is to provide the \emph{forward direction} of the standard smoothness criterion: smoothness of $f$ implies that on every affine chart $V \to U$ (with $V \subseteq X$, $U \subseteq S$ affine and $f V \subseteq U$) the appLE-algebra K\"ahler differential $\Omega_{\Gamma(X, V)\,/\,\Gamma(S, U)}$ is a free $\Gamma(X, V)$-module of rank $n$. This is the Jacobian-criterion direction and is what connects to the dimension hypothesis on the Jacobian: the smoothness of the curve $C \to \Spec k$ in the Jacobian definition is the special case $n = 1$, and the relative dimension of $\Jac(C)$ is read off from the rank via the same forward implication. The relationship between this affine-chart-wise algebra-K\"ahler form and the section module of the relative cotangent presheaf $\Omega_{X/S}$ is the content of milestone~M1 of the project roadmap and is stated as Theorem~\ref{thm:relativeDifferentialsPresheaf_equiv_kaehler_appLE} in Section~\ref{sec:bridge}. The converse implication is \emph{mathematically false} as stated and is documented separately in Section~\ref{sec:converse-out-of-scope}.
46:The forward direction of the textbook smoothness criterion identifies a necessary condition on the relative K\"ahler differentials: every point of $X$ admits an affine chart $V \to U$ over which the appLE-algebra K\"ahler module $\Omega_{B/A}$ is free of the prescribed rank, with $A = \struct{S}(U)$ and $B = \struct{X}(V)$. This is the global version of the algebraic-side fact that an \texttt{Algebra.IsStandardSmoothOfRelativeDimension}~$n$~algebra has a free K\"ahler differential module of rank $n$. We state and prove the theorem directly on the appLE-algebra K\"ahler module, sidestepping the presheaf bridge of Section~\ref{sec:bridge}. The converse implication (a local-freeness hypothesis on $\Omega$ alone implying smoothness) is \emph{false}; see Section~\ref{sec:converse-out-of-scope}.
228:\label{sec:converse-out-of-scope}
266:Each bullet below is a future milestone whose Mathlib infrastructure is not yet available in the pinned snapshot \texttt{b80f227}; inclusion in this list does \emph{not} mean the project has abandoned it. The roadmap progression is: M1 (this iter) $\to$ M2 / M3 (existence routes) $\to$ M4 (converse, Section~\ref{sec:converse-out-of-scope}) $\to$ M5+ (sheafification, cotangent exact sequence, etc., listed here). Each item lists the deferred concept, the missing Mathlib piece, and one cross-reference for forward-looking reinstatement.
Now I'll write all the review outputs.

## Per-target detail

### Target: `AlgebraicGeometry.IsAffineOpen.appLE_isLocalization` (M1.b) — PARTIAL

**Status**: **partial**. Step 0 of the four-step `IsLocalization.of_le`

(bridge body via the M1.d tower-cancellation `LinearEquiv`).

#### Attempt path (chronological; ~53 edits, ~23 diagnostic checks)

1. **L142 module-instance closure** (Edit at L142): replaced
   `letI : Module Γ(X, V) ((relativeDifferentialsPresheaf f).presheaf.obj (.op V)) := sorry`

2. **L109/L112 split via top-level `appLE_colimRingHom` def**: the
   deep-prover subagent introduced a top-level `noncomputable def
   appLE_colimRingHom (f : X ⟶ S) {U V} (e : V ≤ f ⁻¹ᵁ U) : Γ(S, U) ⟶ A_colim`
   as the composition `(adj.unit.app S.presheaf).app (op U) ≫
   ((pullback _ _).obj S.presheaf).map (homOfLE e).op` (L97–L102), and a
   companion `@[reducible] noncomputable def appLE_colimAlgebra` that
   provides the algebra structure as `(appLE_colimRingHom f e).hom.toAlgebra`.
   The body of `appLE_isLocalization` was refactored to use this
   algebra instance via `letI : Algebra Γ(S, U) Acolim := appLE_colimAlgebra f e`
   (replacing the inline `sorry`-ed `letI`). One LSP warning
   ("Definition of class type must be marked with `@[reducible]`")
   was triggered; the fix was already in the writer's pattern
   (`@[reducible]`). **Result: SUCCESS** — L109 sorry eliminated.

3. **Factorisation theorem `appLE_colimRingHom_comp_φV`** (L116–L150,
   ~35 LOC of category-theory manipulation): the prover wrote the
   triangle identity
   `appLE_colimRingHom f e ≫ φV = Scheme.Hom.appLE f U V e`
   where `φV` is `(homEquiv).symm f.c .app (op V)`. The proof
   strategy is the standard unit triangle + naturality combo:
   set `φ' := (adj.homEquiv).symm f.c`, derive
   `htri : adj.unit.app S.presheaf ≫ (pushforward _).map φ' = f.c`
   from `(adj.homEquiv _ _).apply_symm_apply f.c`; specialise at
   `op U` to get
   `htriU : (adj.unit.app S.presheaf).app (op U) ≫ φ'.app (op (f ⁻¹ᵁ U)) = Scheme.Hom.app f U`;
   combine with `φ'.naturality (homOfLE e).op` via a 3-step `calc`
   that swaps the unit-naturality `≫` and reassociates. The proof
   required ~6 attempts to land:
   - early attempts via `rw [show ... from ...]` failed because
     the `((pullback ⋙ pushforward).obj S.presheaf)` rewrite target
     does not pattern-match against `(((pullback _).obj S.presheaf).obj _)`
     under the rewriter's type-equality elaboration (functor-composition
     defeq blocker);
   - the calc-block approach succeeded by sequencing the rewrites
     into explicit `=` steps that each unify pointwise rather than
     leaning on `rw`'s pattern-match. **Result: SUCCESS** (after iteration).

4. **L145 bridge body M1.e closure** (single Edit): the body of
   `relativeDifferentialsPresheaf_equiv_kaehler_appLE` is closed by:
   - `letI algSU_colim := appLE_colimAlgebra f e` (the cocone leg
     algebra),
   - `haveI hLoc := appLE_isLocalization f hU hV e` (the M1.b
     hypothesis — used as a black box since the body is still open),
   - `letI algColim_B := φV.hom.toAlgebra` (the upper Algebra layer),
   - `letI algSU_B := (Scheme.Hom.appLE f U V e).hom.toAlgebra` (the
     overall appLE Algebra layer),
   - `haveI hTower : IsScalarTower Γ(S, U) Acolim Γ(X, V) := IsScalarTower.of_algebraMap_eq' <| by ...`
     via the factorisation `appLE_colimRingHom_comp_φV`,
   - `exact (kaehler_quotient_localization_iso _).symm` (the M1.d
     `LinearEquiv`).
   This packaging closes the M1.e bridge body modulo M1.b — the
   `IsLocalization` hypothesis is invoked by name; the bridge is
   structurally complete and will become fully `\leanok`-able as
   soon as `appLE_isLocalization` closes. **Result: SUCCESS** (first
   probe via the new helper chain).

5. **Step 0 lemma `isUnit_appLE_unitSubmonoid_in_colim`** (L164–L267,
   ~70 LOC) — the iter's hardest piece of work. The goal is to show
   that each `g ∈ appLE_unitSubmonoid f hU hV e` is a unit in `A_colim`
   under the `appLE_colimAlgebra` algebra map. Strategy
   (5-substep, all closed):
   - **(a)** From `hg : IsUnit ((Scheme.Hom.appLE f U V e).hom g)` derive
     `hVle : V ≤ f ⁻¹ᵁ S.basicOpen g` using `Scheme.basicOpen_of_isUnit`
     + `Scheme.basicOpen_appLE` (which says
     `X.basicOpen ((f.appLE V U e).hom s) = V ⊓ f ⁻¹ᵁ (S.basicOpen s)`).
   - **(b)** Build the cocone leg `cleg : Γ(S, S.basicOpen g) ⟶ Acolim`
     as `(adj.unit.app S.presheaf).app (op (S.basicOpen g)) ≫ Pf.map
     (homOfLE hVle).op` (the "pre-image-presheaf restriction at the
     `S.basicOpen g` index"), and the restriction
     `rstr : Γ(S, U) ⟶ Γ(S, S.basicOpen g)` as `S.presheaf.map (homOfLE
     (S.basicOpen_le g)).op`.
   - **(c)** Prove `hcompat : appLE_colimRingHom f e = rstr ≫ cleg`.
     This is the "naturality + functoriality" identity. Proof: factor
     `homOfLE e = homOfLE (f.preimage_le_of_le (S.basicOpen_le g)) ≫
     homOfLE hVle` as ops in `(Opens X)ᵒᵖ`, apply `Pf.map_comp` (where
     `Pf := (pullback _ _).obj S.presheaf`), then commute the unit
     restriction via naturality of `adj.unit.app S.presheaf` at
     `homOfLE (S.basicOpen_le g)`. **This step took ~15 edit attempts**
     because `rw [Functor.map_comp]` failed pervasively on
     `((pullback _ _).obj S.presheaf).map (f ≫ g)` despite the pattern
     being printed in the failure message. Diagnosis from
     `lean_diagnostic_messages`: pattern-match runs before defeq
     elaboration, and the `Lan`-defined functor instance carries
     category metadata that doesn't unify under the rewriter.
     **Workaround**: pre-prove `hmc : Pf.map (f ≫ g) = Pf.map f ≫ Pf.map g`
     as a `have ... := Functor.map_comp _ _ _`, then `erw [hmc]`
     (`erw` succeeds where `rw` fails). Final form at L208–L247.
   - **(d)** Use `IsAffineOpen.isLocalization_basicOpen hU g` to get
     `IsLocalization.Away g Γ(S, S.basicOpen g)`, then
     `IsLocalization.Away.algebraMap_isUnit g` gives
     `h_unit_rest : IsUnit ((algebraMap Γ(S, U) Γ(S, S.basicOpen g)) g)`.
     Used `h_alg_rest : (algebraMap _ _) = rstr.hom := rfl` to convert
     to ring-hom form for the next step.
   - **(e)** Push through the cocone leg: define
     `h_factor : (appLE_colimRingHom f e).hom g = cleg.hom (rstr.hom g)`
     via `rw [hcompat, CommRingCat.hom_comp]; rfl`, then conclude
     `IsUnit ((appLE_colimRingHom f e).hom g)` by `rw [h_factor]; exact
     h_unit_rest.map cleg.hom`. The final step then exploits the
     definitional equality `(algebraMap Γ(S, U) Acolim) = (appLE_colimRingHom f e).hom`
     (from `appLE_colimAlgebra := φ.hom.toAlgebra`) to `exact h_unit_colim`.
     **Critical lesson**: `change`/`show` on `algebraMap` failed
     directly (`'change' tactic failed, pattern ... is not definitionally
     equal to target`) — `Lean's elaboration of IsUnit and the algebra
     instance aligns when the term is exact-ed rather than change-d`.
     Workaround: route the conclusion through `IsUnit ((appLE_colimRingHom f e).hom g)`
     first via the `h_factor` hypothesis, then close via `exact`.

   **Result: SUCCESS** — Step 0 closure landed at L267, ~70 LOC.

6. **L304 `appLE_isLocalization` body — Steps 1–4 NOT attempted this iter.**
   The body remains a single `sorry` at L304. The plan-phase
   PROGRESS.md framed PARTIAL as the realistic outcome, so this
   matches expectations. Estimated remaining LOC: 100–250 (per the
   `task_results/Differentials.lean.md` analysis).

## Key findings / proof patterns discovered this session

1. **`rw [Functor.map_comp]` fails on `((TopCat.Presheaf.pullback _ _).obj _).map (f ≫ g)`**
   despite the pattern being literally present in the goal. Diagnosis:
   the `Lan`-defined functor instance carries category-instance
   metadata that the rewriter's unification doesn't see through.
   **Workaround**: pre-prove `hmc : Pf.map (f ≫ g) = Pf.map f ≫ Pf.map g`
   as a `have ... := Functor.map_comp _ _ _`, then `erw [hmc]`.

2. **`rw [Category.assoc]` similarly fails** in goals involving the
   left-Kan-extended functor. **Workaround**: `exact Category.assoc _ _ _`
   directly when the goal is exactly the associativity equation.

3. **`change`/`show` on `algebraMap g` doesn't unify** even when the
   algebra is `φ.hom.toAlgebra` (which should make
   `algebraMap = φ.hom` definitionally). **Workaround**: route the
   conclusion through `IsUnit ((appLE_colimRingHom f e).hom g)` first
   via a `have h_factor`, then `exact` the result.

4. **`adj.unit.naturality` produces an equation involving the identity
   functor** `((𝟭 _).obj _)`. **Workaround**: `simpa using
   ((adj.unit.app S.presheaf)).naturality _` cleans it up.

5. **Pattern for "directed colim of localizations is localization at
   union submonoid"**: build it as `IsLocalization` via
   `IsLocalization.of_le`, not as a Mathlib `Functor.Final` colim
   comparison (already a Knowledge Base pattern entry from iter-121;
   reconfirmed this iter that Step 0 was the right initial step).

(Full list in `recommendations.md`. Top-priority items:

# Recommendations for the next plan-agent iteration (iter-123)

## CRITICAL — must be acted on this iter

### CRITICAL #1 — Continue M1.b: iter-123 prover lane on `appLE_isLocalization` (Steps 1–4)

**Target**: `AlgebraicJacobian/Differentials.lean:282` `appLE_isLocalization`
body (residual `sorry` at L304).

**Status**: Step 0 of the 4-step `IsLocalization.of_le` chain is
closed this iter (`isUnit_appLE_unitSubmonoid_in_colim`, L164, ~70 LOC).
Steps 1–4 remain. The plan-agent's iter-122 PROGRESS.md framed
PARTIAL as expected; the structural-advance criterion fires (Step 0
closed + 3 of 4 sorry sites eliminated + bridge body L145 closed
modulo M1.b), so this is the canonical CONVERGING route.

**Recommended action**: dispatch a prover on `Differentials.lean` with
PROGRESS.md objectives targeting Steps 1–4 in order. The remaining
~100–250 LOC decomposes:

- **Step 1 (~30–60 LOC)**: build the universal map
  `Localization (appLE_unitSubmonoid f hU hV e) → A_colim` via
  `IsLocalization.lift (appLE_colimRingHom f e).hom` using
  `isUnit_appLE_unitSubmonoid_in_colim` as the
  unit-of-image hypothesis (the `IsLocalization.lift` signature is
  `(g : M → S₁) (hg : ∀ y, IsUnit (g y)) : S → S₁`; the Step 0
  helper feeds the `hg` slot directly).

- **Step 2 (~50–120 LOC, hardest)**: build the reverse map
  `A_colim → A_M` via the cocone universal property of the
  pullback presheaf's `Lan` construction. Strategy: each
  `Γ(S, W) → A_M` for `f V ⊆ W ⊆ U` factors through some
  `Γ(S, D(g)) = (Γ(S, U))_g` via quasi-compactness of `f V` + a
  basic-open cover; the universal arrow then assembles via
  `IsColimit.desc`. This is the only step that needs scheme-theoretic
  cofinality reasoning; the rest is pure ring-theory.

- **Step 3 (~10–30 LOC)**: composite-identity verification via
  `IsLocalization.ringHom_ext` (`Mathlib.RingTheory.Localization.Defs`)
  + `IsColimit.hom_ext` on the colimit cocone. The
  `appLE_colimRingHom_comp_φV` factorisation theorem (L116, fully
  proved) is the right input here — both composites land as
  `Scheme.Hom.appLE` after applying the factorisation, so the
  `ringHom_ext` reduces to a single arrow check.

- **Step 4 (~5–10 LOC)**: package the resulting
  `Localization M ≃ₐ[Γ(S, U)] A_colim` via
  `IsLocalization.isLocalization_of_algEquiv` (or `IsLocalization.of_le`
  per the analogist's recommendation).

**Reusable inputs from iter-122**:
- `isUnit_appLE_unitSubmonoid_in_colim` (L164, fully proved) — Step 1 input.
- `appLE_colimRingHom_comp_φV` (L116, fully proved) — Step 3 input.
- `appLE_colimRingHom` (L97), `appLE_colimAlgebra` (L106) — Step 1/2 algebra.

**Effort estimate**: 1 prover iter, 100–250 LOC.

**Risk profile**: Step 2 is the only step with genuine novelty. If
Step 2 alone takes >2 prover iters, dispatch `mathlib-analogist`
on the cocone-universal-property usage before continuing — the
project hasn't previously had to do scheme-theoretic `IsColimit.desc`
work, so this is in fresh territory where the analogist's
"BEFORE the design ships" guidance applies.

436–447) claims the presheaf-form bridge is "**a Mathlib gap** ...

## MEDIUM — should be acted on within 2-3 iters

### MEDIUM #5 — Document the "rw [Functor.map_comp] needs erw on Lan-defined functors" workaround

The Knowledge Base in `PROJECT_STATUS.md` has entries about cofinality
framing and `FormallyUnramified.of_isLocalization`; it does NOT yet
